Patch Set 1:

Rosso Autotest has started the test process
Patch Set 1: Looks good to me, approved

Patch Set 1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(1 inline comment)

I had no idea that autoconf would rewrite an #undef like that.  Good job figuring that out!  I think that is a definite improvement.

But I think we need to work on the location of the header, and how it gets installed.  As the patch stands now, I think that ldiskfs_config.h is only a temporary file that lives in the build directory.

ldiskfs_config.h needs to be installed with all the rest of the headers as part of "make install".  That way it can be packaged into the ldiskfs-devel package in the future.  (I already create an ldiskfs-devel package in my work-in-progress LU-1199 series).
Uploaded patch set 2.

Patch Set 2:

Build Started http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/13936/

Patch Set 2: Verified

Build Successful 
 
http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/13936/ : SUCCESS

Patch Set 2:

Toro has started the test process

Patch Set 1:

Test abandoned because later commit 13936 found

Patch Set 1:

Test abandoned because later commit 13936 found

Patch Set 2:

Maybe I'm missing something, but I don't see where this header is installed as Chris had asked?

Patch Set 2:

Oh I see. I address only one of his comments. So he wants to ensure that ldisk_config.h is installed with make install

Patch Set 2:

Okay I'm confused by what exactly Chris wants. I moved ldiskfs_config.h to be in the same directory as all the other ldiskfs headers. Looking at the source module shows it there as well. The makefile appear to do the right thing. What am I missing?

Patch Set 2:

Do this:  cd ldiskfs && sh autogen.sh && ./configure && sudo make install

Careful with the sudo.  Even if you use DESTDIR or something, make install wants to put the modules in /lib/modules.

Where does lustre_config.h get installed?

Patch Set 2:

And the answer might be that it gets installed in the same place as the other headers.  Moving the header down one level into ldiskfs/ldiksfs may have been enough to get it packaged with the other headers.  I haven't tested it yet.

Patch Set 2:

Ah, yes, I see that the install-data-local target in ldiskfs/ldiskfs/Makefile.in uses a wildcard on header files:

instfiles=$$(find . -name '*.h' | grep -v linux-stage);

So I think it is likely that moving ldiskfs_config.h to ldiskfs/ldiskfs addressed my requirement.

Patch Set 2:

I uncommented the install-data-local and ran it. Yep ldiskfs-config.h got installed along side the other ldiskfs headers.

Patch Set 2: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(2 inline comments)

Awesome!  Thanks for checking that.  No need to uncomment install-data-local in this patch; it is enough to know that is in the right place and ready for my later patches.

There are a couple of minor notes inline.

The one bigger problem is that now Lustre is not using ldiskfs_config.h at all, since the -include was removed.

Like I said, I like the the idea of an additional patch in the ldiskfs series to explicitly add the "#include <ldiskfs/ldisfkfs_config.h>" so that ldiskfs takes care of this entirely on its own.  But I don't know if others will agree with me on that.  I won't put up a fight if you want to do it another way.  But we'll need something. :)
Uploaded patch set 3.

Patch Set 5:

No, but we don't yet have an ldiskfs testbed for the pre-2.4 stuff.  I'm pushing hard to get that created ASAP, but the hardware is otherwise occupied.

If the failures are really from this patch, my guess would be that we missed some places in lustre where ldiskfs defines need to be set.  I made that mistake in one of my early patch sets, and saw somewhat similar varied failures.

When I reviewed your patch I didn't notice any places that you missed, but maybe I just missed them too. :)

parents.subject: LU-3035 clio: improper LASSERT in ll_file_io_generic()

commit.subject: LU-1199 build: Cleanup ldiskfs kernel config defines
commit.message:
LU-1199 build: Cleanup ldiskfs kernel config defines

Make it more clear what the purpose of the "CONFIG_LDISKFS_*"
defines are with a comment, and separate them from options
that do not necessarily originate in the kernel source. All of
these options are needed for ldiskfs and some are needed by
osd-ldiskfs in the lustre file system. To handle these shared
options a extra autoconf header file is create containing only
the values of interest to osd-ldiskfs.

Signed-off-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@gmail.com>
Change-Id: I8e71b6cf471c4317b20fdce14d66f8b2883a226e
